Corrected Summary Based On Agent Reasoning Only:

Keratocysts are odontogenic cysts commonly located in the jawbone where chronic inflammation produces abundant exudative fluid rich in soluble proteins. Aspiration of these cysts yields a high level of soluble proteins secondary to persistent local inflammation and secretory activity within the lesion.
